data.title: Gemini Usage Explodes as API Calls More than Double in Five Months
data.description:
Tech giant Google (GOOGL) is starting to see clearer financial benefits from the recent improvements to its Gemini AI models. In fact, Gemini usage has exploded, with API calls more than doubling from March to August as newer model versions like Gemini 2.5 and Gemini 3 gained strong developer traction, according to The Information. Additionally, that growth is not only increasing direct AI-related revenue but also encouraging customers to spend more on other Google Cloud services, such as storage and databases.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, these gains are expected to become more visible in Google's upcoming fourth-quarter earnings, which are scheduled for early February. Unsurprisingly, investors will be watching closely to see whether Google can justify the massive investments it has made in AI, including capital spending that nearly doubled year-over-year. Furthermore, alongside infrastructure buildouts, the company has been hiring aggressively across its Google Cloud division and its AI research arm, DeepMind.

Nevertheless, Google's biggest challenge remains turning that momentum into higher-margin software sales. This is because while Gemini Enterprise, its premium AI software suite, has grown to millions of subscribers, customer feedback has been mixed. More precisely, some businesses praise its ability to search internal data and assist with research, while others find it less effective for building complex, customized applications.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 27 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $342.39 per share implies 3.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Riot Platforms Stock (RIOT) Scores Analysts' Bullish Reviews on AI Deal with AMD
data.description:
Riot Platforms (RIOT) stock jumped 16.1% on Friday after the Bitcoin (BTC-USD) miner secured a 10-year, $311 million deal with chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to lease 25 megawatts of data center capacity. Several Wall Street analysts reacted positively to the news, as it reflects the company's ability to pivot from Bitcoin mining to operating data centers amid a surge in high-performance computing (HPC) to support the artificial intelligence (AI) boom.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that AMD holds an option for an additional 75 megawatts of capacity and a right of first refusal (ROFR) for another 100 megawatts.

Analysts Are Upbeat about Riot Platforms' Deal with AMD  

Following the AI deal, Piper Sandler analyst Patrick Moley reiterated a Buy rating on Riot Platforms stock with a price target of $26. The analyst noted that Riot Platforms signed its first AI and HPC deal with an initial capacity of 25 MW and an option to extend the lease capacity further. Moley thinks that the deal "establishes RIOT as a credible data center option for hyperscaler tenants."

Furthermore, Moley views this deal as an encouraging initial step toward Riot Platforms eventually contracting all the 1.2 GW critical IT across the Rockdale and Corsicana sites. The analyst believes a full transition of these sites to AI/HPC data centers could translate to over $30 per share in value for RIOT based on management's run-rate NOI (net operating income) projections.

Likewise, BTIG analyst Gregory Lewis reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $28. The 5-star analyst noted that an expansion option for 75 MW and a ROFR on an additional 100 MW would represent revenue of about $2.2 billion over 10 years, though pricing could differ owing to the larger scale of the additional build-outs. While RIOT has secured an HPC customer at Rockdale, Lewis stated that he always viewed the company's 1 GW of power infrastructure capacity at their Corsicana site as the more attractive potential HPC data center location.

Also, Roth Capital analyst Darren Aftahi reaffirmed a Buy rating on RIOT stock with a price target of $42. Aftahi stated that the company's first HPC lease with AMD is a crucial pivot. While the deal is modest in size, the 4-star analyst noted that it offers a "very favorable" high-teens adjusted yield on cost and fast monetization. The analyst believes that the lease establishes Riot Platforms' credibility, as the company targets larger Tier 3 builds in Corsicana or the remainder of the Rockdale site.

Is RIOT Stock a Buy or Sell?

Interestingly, Riot Platforms scores a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 15 unanimous Buys. The average RIOT stock price target of $27.71 indicates 44% upside potential. RIOT stock has rallied 52% year-to-date.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Gold Futures Shine as Trump's Greenland Tariffs Spark Sharp EU Pushback
data.description:
Gold futures are up by over 2% on Monday as investors digest a new tariff announcement from President Trump.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Effective January 1, eight European countries, including Denmark, Germany, and the United Kingdom, will face an additional 10% tariff until the U.S. signs a deal to purchase Greenland. The move has heightened geopolitical tensions and benefited gold, which is a safe-haven asset and tends to rise during periods of uncertainty.

Why is Gold Trading Higher Today?

Various leaders across the European Union have spoken out against the duties. On Sunday, the eight countries impacted by the tariffs released a joint statement, reiterating their support for Greenland and Denmark. "Tariff threats undermine transatlantic relations and risk triggering a dangerous negative spiral," read the statement. "We will continue to remain united and coordinated in our response."

Last year, gold delivered its best performance since 1979, and the precious metal is off to a fantastic start in 2026 with an 8% return.

data.title: Americans Pay for 96% of Trump's Tariffs, Kiel Study Shows
data.description:
Americans pay for an overwhelming majority of President Trump's tariffs, according to a study released by the Kiel Institute that analyzed shipment-level data across 25 million transactions worth nearly $4 trillion.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The 2025 US tariffs are an own goal: American importers and consumers bear nearly the entire cost," the German research firm said. "Foreign exporters absorb only about 4% of the tariff burden—the remaining 96% is passed through to US buyers."

U.S. Tariffs Hit Domestic Consumers the Hardest, Says Kiel

Kiel conducted event studies on Brazil and India, two countries that the U.S. imposed outsized tariff rates on. The firm concluded that the tariffs didn't cause export prices to fall. Instead, they reduced trade volume. Export data from India confirmed the observation, with Indian exporters maintaining prices and decreasing shipments.

U.S. consumers are responsible for the brunt of the tariff burden, according to Kiel. The firm added that tariffs disrupt supply chains and consumption patterns while reducing the variety of products available to buyers. "The $200 billion surge in customs revenue represents $200 billion extracted from American businesses and households," Kiel said.

data.title: Are the Magnificent 7 Stocks No Longer Magnificent? 'The Correlation Has Fallen Apart,' Says Bahnsen CIO
data.description:
The Magnificent 7, a group of seven of the largest companies in the world, including Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOGL), posted a disappointing performance in 2025. Within the group, only NVDA and GOOGL outperformed the S&P 500 (SPX) during the year amid fears of overspending in AI and falling profit margins.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The correlation has fallen apart," said Bahnsen Group CIO David Bahnsen. "What they have in common is being trillion-dollar companies."

Magnificent 7 Faces Pressure as AI Expands Beyond Tech

Another factor affecting the Magnificent 7's disappointing performance is a broadening of the AI theme into sectors beyond technology, such as energy, healthcare, and biotechnology. At the same time, the group still accounts for an outsized portion of the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX). That could trigger elevated market volatility if the Magnificent 7 declines in unison.

"There is not a suitable replacement yet," said State Street Chief Investment Strategist Michael Arone. "But I think there probably will be."

The group has also begun falling out of favor with retail investors. Retail trading volume in the seven stocks, as a percent of overall trading volume, has shrunk since 2023 and 2024, according to Vanda Research.

data.title: The Stock Market Is Closed on MLK Day, but Here's What Goldman Sachs Says to Buy When Trading Resumes
data.description:
The stock market isn't open on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, but that doesn't mean investors are sitting idle. While markets are closed in observance of the holiday, Wall Street's focus is already shifting toward what could be top trades once trading resumes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

One name drawing attention from Goldman Sachs analyst Asad Haider is BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX). On Friday, Haider upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy and lifted his 12-month price target to $142 from $115, implying 30% upside from current levels.

Haider believes BioNTech sits "at the nexus of significant developments occurring in the oncology field," pointing to the company's expanding footprint across next-generation immuno-oncology and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). In Haider's view, that positioning could give BioNTech a meaningful role in what the analyst describes as the "next wave" of cancer treatment innovation – well beyond the company's legacy COVID-19 vaccine business.

Importantly, the bullish call isn't built around a single asset or trial. Haider highlights BioNTech's broad, strategic portfolio, which includes novel PD-1/VEGF bispecifics, ADC programs, and mRNA-based cancer vaccines. The analyst argues this diversified approach allows the company to pursue multiple shots on goal across large oncology indications, while also reducing dependence on any single readout.

While COVID-related revenue continues to generate meaningful cash flow, Haider believes the oncology pipeline is set to become the more material driver of stock performance. The analyst notes that BioNTech shares have lagged in 2025, in part due to lingering overhang from the COVID franchise. However, he sees the narrative beginning to shift as the company moves deeper into late-stage development, with a series of clinical milestones expected to build into 2026.

In fact, Haider points to 2026 as the start of a multi-year catalyst-rich period, with BioNTech expecting Phase 2 and Phase 3 data readouts across its oncology programs. In his framework, success does not need to be binary; progress in individual indications could independently unlock value and steadily rebuild investor confidence.

Haider argues that BioNTech's current share price effectively values the company close to its cash and COVID business alone, assigning limited credit to its oncology assets. In his view, that creates what he describes as "asymmetric upside," particularly if upcoming data begin to validate the pipeline's breadth and potential.

"We believe BNTX has strategically positioned itself to be a leader in this next wave of potential advancements, presenting a unique risk/reward opportunity," the analyst summed up.

Overall, BNTX has picked up 15 recent analyst reviews, and these break down to 12 Buys and 3 Holds to support the Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are priced at $109 and have an average target price of $141.93, which is practically the same as Haider's. (See BNTX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Move Over, Citadel. Chris Hohn's TCI Rakes in Record $18.9 Billion in Profits
data.description:
Ken Griffin's Citadel once held the record for the highest annual profit by a hedge fund, pulling in $16 billion in 2022. However, a new billionaire now holds that record.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Chris Hohn's The Children's Investment (TCI) Fund raked in $18.9 billion in net profits in 2025, according to Swiss investment firm Edmond de Rothschild. Since its inception in 2003, TCI has delivered $68.4 billion in returns post fees.

What Stocks Does TCI Own?

TCI operates as a highly concentrated firm with a long-term focus. Its latest 13F filing showed $52.66 billion split across just nine holdings:

  1. General Electric (GE): 27.12%
  2. Visa (V): 18.18%
  3. Microsoft (MSFT): 16.31%
  4. Moody's Corp (MCO): 12.03%
  5. S&P Global (SPGI): 10.33%
  6. Canadian Pacific Kansas City (CP): 7.05%
  7. Google (GOOG): 3.51%
  8. Canadian National Railway (CNI): 3.36%
  9. Ferrovial SE (FER): 2.11%

TCI's total assets under management (AUM) stand at $77.1 billion. The hedge fund's master fund returned 27.8% in 2025, beating the S&P 500's (SPX) return of 17.9%.

data.title: Trump's Greenland Tariffs Hit with EU 'Trade Bazooka' Threat
data.description:
The European Union is considering using a powerful legal measure in response to President Trump's 10% tariff on eight European countries. The tariffs will remain in effect until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the weekend, French President Emmanuel Macron weighed the idea of invoking the European Union's "trade bazooka," or the anti-coercion instrument (ACI). The ACI can be activated in response to situations involving economic coercion, which is defined as actions by a third country seeking to pressure the EU or its member states into changing policy through trade or investment measures.

What is the Anti-Coercion Instrument?

Once activated, the ACI allows the EU to impose retaliatory tariffs, restrict market access, and levy additional taxes on foreign companies. The ACI was adopted in 2023 as a measure to address tariffs from the U.S. and China.

Other measures are being considered by the 27-nation bloc, including the revival of a $103 billion tariff package on U.S. goods. EU leaders are set to meet this Thursday for an emergency summit in Brussels to discuss their options.

data.title: 'Don't Be a Hater,' Says Top Investor About Super Micro Computer Stock
data.description:
The verdict was swift and painful last October for Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) when the company shared that its Q1 2026 revenues had hit a bump in the road. In a note released just a few days prior to its earnings call, SMCI reported that "design wins" had pushed some expected sales for Q1 into the following quarter.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While some companies might receive the benefit of the doubt when sharing a hiccup or two, clearly SMCI isn't one of them. Though things have improved a bit in 2026, SMCI's share price has lost some 40% of its value over the past three months.

In a sense, it seems that SMCI has yet to fully recover from the negative vibes around its previous accounting and compliance issues. However, one top investor known by the pseudonym Stone Fox Capital doesn't think that SMCI deserves to be "the most hated AI stock."

"Investors should use the weakness to load up on the stock with the ability to both beat FY27 targets and rerate based on the boosted numbers," states the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Stone Fox points out that the demand for data centers remains piping hot. The investor cites a recent announcement from Meta Platforms' CEO Mark Zuckerberg, who shared his company's intention to build hundreds of GW or more as part of its "Meta Compute" initiative.

Moreover, McKinsey & Company has forecasted that data center capacity will surge from 82 GW in 2025 to 219 GW in 2030, notes Stone Fox. The investor also mentions that much of this spend will be on technologies that SMCI is working on.

The huge capex spend meshes with SMCI's forecast for future orders, further details Stone Fox. Therefore, Stone Fox expects the company to beat what the investor deems "relatively flat" revenue projections in the coming two years.

"My investment thesis remains ultra-bullish on the stock, especially after falling back to yearly lows despite ongoing strong AI server demand," sums up Stone Fox, who rates SMCI a Buy. (To watch Stone Fox Capital's track record, click here)

Wall Street, as Stone Fox mentions, isn't exactly showering SMCI with love. With 5 Buys, 5 Holds, and 2 Sells, SMCI carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $44.82 would translate into gains of ~37%. (See SMCI stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: 'It's Not Too Late,' Says Top Investor About Intel Stock
data.description:
There are plenty of superlatives that could describe the path of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) during 2025. The company flipped the script on a disappointing period, delivering an impressive set of achievements that included a new partnership with Nvidia and a strategic alignment (and investment) from the U.S. government.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier this month, Intel launched its Core Ultra Series 3 processors, which the company trumpeted as "the most advanced semiconductor process ever developed and manufactured in the United States."

The company's share price has skyrocketed over the past year, more than doubling during the past twelve months. The burner has continued this year as well, and INTC is up by almost 30% in the first few weeks of 2026.

On the heels of such a striking reversal of fortune – and just before the company shares its Q4 2025 numbers on January 22 – is it too late to jump on board? Not according to top investor Timothy Green, who believes INTC still has room to run.

"While shares of Intel have surged since the start of 2025, the company's market capitalization is still well below its all-time high, and even below multi-year highs reached in early 2020," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Green highlights three main drivers of future growth: the launch of Panther Lake, the demand for its server CPUs, and the company's long-term foundry opportunity.

Regarding Panther Lake, the investor cites the improved performance and efficiency of the new chip, along with its enhanced battery life and "huge leap in graphics capabilities." Green notes that Nova Lake is slated to follow Panther Lake, and he also mentions the potential Serpent Lake PC CPU that is rumored to be the codename for Intel's joint efforts with Nvidia.

Another factor behind the investor's optimism is due to improvements in the company's CPU offerings. Green points out that Intel has nearly sold out its server CPUs for 2026.

"With strong demand and major performance and efficiency improvements likely this year and next as Intel launches new chips, the company should see solid revenue and profit growth in its data center segment," adds Green.

Lastly, the investor cites the opportunity for Intel's foundry to generate meaningful business from external customers, though he also acknowledges that this will take some time.

Therefore, though he warns that it won't always be smooth sailing, Green is along for the long-term ride.

"Intel stock has shot up quickly, and volatility will likely be par for the course. But in the long run, Intel looks like a great investment as the company stages a comeback," concludes Green. (To watch Timothy Green's track record, click here)

That's far from the consensus view on Wall Street, however, which seems to think that the upward momentum will be slowing down. With 8 Buys, 19 Holds, and 4 Sells, INTC carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $43.37 implies a potential downside of ~8% in the year ahead. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross
